frames.name: Constitutional Victory Frame
frames.description: Presents the story as law firms successfully defending constitutional principles against government overreach
frames.emphasis: Law firms' principled stand, constitutional protections, and initial DOJ capitulation as vindication of rule of law

frames.name: Administrative Chaos Frame
frames.description: Focuses on the DOJ's confusing back-and-forth decisions as evidence of internal dysfunction
frames.emphasis: The unexplained reversal, lack of coherent strategy, and embarrassing flip-flop within 24 hours

frames.name: Power Struggle Frame
frames.description: Frames the conflict as ongoing battle between Trump administration and legal profession
frames.emphasis: Continued fight despite setbacks, administration's determination to pursue executive orders

frames.name: Legal Process Frame
frames.description: Presents story through procedural lens of court filings, appeals, and legal technicalities
frames.emphasis: Court procedures, filing deadlines, and appellate process mechanics

outlierPerspectives: UPI uniquely includes expert analysis suggesting the reversal may be motivated by fear of losing deals with other law firms
UPI alone provides extensive quotes from legal experts calling the actions 'embarrassing' and 'unconstitutional'
The Guardian US uniquely characterizes other firms' settlements as 'capitalistic cowardice'
missingContext: Specific details about what the executive orders would have actually done beyond general descriptions
Timeline of when the original executive orders were issued and legal challenges began
Details about the $1 billion in pro bono work agreements with the nine firms that settled
Explanation of why Friday was the deadline for DOJ brief filing
Background on what specific clients or causes these firms represented that triggered Trump's targeting
Analysis of the legal precedent or constitutional basis for the judges' rulings
